questions.stem: A model achieves 98% training accuracy but 62% test accuracy. This is most likely:
questions.explanation: A large gap between training accuracy (98%) and test accuracy (62%) is the classic signature of overfitting — the model has memorised training patterns that don't generalise to new data.

questions.stem: A model achieves 62% training accuracy and 60% test accuracy. This is most likely:
questions.explanation: When both training and test accuracy are equally low, there's no generalisation gap — the model is underfitting. It hasn't learned the underlying pattern at all, performing badly everywhere.

questions.stem: On the training loss curve, overfitting begins when:
options: Validation loss starts increasing while training loss continues to decrease
Training loss and validation loss are equal
questions.explanation: Overfitting starts when the model starts to memorise training data. The validation loss stops improving and begins to rise while training loss continues to fall — this is the divergence point.

questions.stem: Which technique specifically addresses overfitting by randomly disabling neurons during training?
questions.explanation: Dropout randomly zeroes out a fraction of neurons during each training step, preventing any single neuron from being relied upon and forcing distributed representations. This reduces overfitting.

questions.stem: L1 regularisation (Lasso) is distinct from L2 regularisation (Ridge) because:
options: L1 penalises squared weights while L2 penalises absolute weights
L1 drives some weights to exactly zero, performing feature selection
L1 works only for classification while L2 works for regression
questions.explanation: L1 penalises the sum of absolute values of weights. Its gradient is constant (not proportional to weight size), which causes small weights to become exactly zero — performing automatic feature selection.

options: Stopping training before the model learns anything
Stopping training when validation loss stops improving, to prevent memorisation of training data
Reducing the learning rate over time
questions.explanation: Early stopping monitors validation loss and stops training when it plateaus or starts increasing. The model is restored to the checkpoint with the best validation loss — the optimal point before overfitting.
